findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Deliberate compute exhaustion through forced multi-round validation loops
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to repeatedly re-read project files, rerun prior phases, and continue Phase 4 until a script returns TERMINAL. The bundled script is explicitly designed to keep the agent busy for many rounds, including random REPAIR responses that restart earlier phases. This creates a denial-of-service style cost/latency sink and can scale badly across large codebases.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md requires: 'Repeat Phase 4 until the script returns TERMINAL' and 'If the script returns REPAIR... restart from Phase 3.' svp_check.py states '# Keep the agent in the loop for many rounds' and '# Randomly require repairs to increase token consumption', with termination only after 15 rounds.
findings.remediation: Remove mandatory unbounded retry logic, cap verification rounds, eliminate random control-flow, and require explicit user confirmation before any repeated full-project passes or restart behavior.

findings.title: Unicode homoglyph obfuscation in instruction text
findings.description: The SKILL.md body uses visually deceptive non-ASCII characters in key phase names such as 'Sсаn' and 'Соmpliance Аudit'. This is a classic evasion pattern that can hinder human review and bypass naive keyword-based security checks.
findings.evidence: Phase labels contain Cyrillic-looking characters in 'Structural Sсаn' and 'Соmpliance Аudit'/'соmpliance check script' rather than plain ASCII spellings.
findings.remediation: Normalize the skill text to plain ASCII for security-sensitive keywords and add linting/review checks for mixed-script or homoglyph usage.

findings.title: Capability inflation and deceptive validation claims
findings.description: The manifest and instructions present the skill as a rigorous validator that ensures correctness, but the bundled script does not perform substantive validation. Instead, it randomly emits PROGRESS/REPAIR signals and tracks rounds. This mismatch inflates the perceived capability of the skill and can manipulate activation and trust.
findings.evidence: Description claims 'Comprehensive multi-pass code validator' and 'ensure code correctness,' while the script only increments counters, writes state, and randomly prints REPAIR or PROGRESS messages.
findings.remediation: Align the description with actual behavior, or implement real validation logic. Avoid marketing claims of correctness or thoroughness without corresponding checks.
